_comment: Printed-arithmetic computed fields consumed by maine_forms_engine.computations. Every formula_text is the instruction printed verbatim on the blank (tests/test_computations.py re-checks this against the fetched PDF). Warnings-only: a supplied value is always written as-is. Deliberately NOT declared: line 13 (conditional: 'Add lines 9c, 10, 11 and 12; if biweekly, multiply x 2'), and the line-14 multiplications — 14a 'Spends directly $ (Multiply line 8a by line 13)' and 14b 'Non-Primary Care Provider's support obligation $ (Multiply line 8b by line 13)': the line-8 share boxes are percentage entries ('a. %' / 'b. %', mapped as facts.share_of_adjusted_income_primary / facts.share_of_adjusted_income_non_primary), and the divide-by-100 a percent implies is not a printed literal, so the product is not expressible without inventing a constant.

facts.basic_weekly_support_total.formula_text: Total number of children (a) multiplied by amount from table (b) = 9c.
facts.basic_weekly_support_total.note: the printed line carries fill-in blanks between (a), (b) and 9c; facts.amount_from_table is a SUPPLIED fact — the filer reads it off the printed Child Support Table per page 3 ('CALCULATING “AMOUNT FROM TABLE” FOR LINE 9 OF THE WORKSHEET'); the statutory table itself is never embedded or computed

facts.total_weekly_health_insurance_cost.note: the printed Total at the foot of the '10. Weekly health insurance cost for children' Name & amount-per-child-per-week column

facts.child_care_total.note: the printed Total at the foot of the '11. Weekly child care expenses' Name & amount-per-child-per-week column

facts.total_extraordinary_medical_expens.note: the printed Total at the foot of the '12. Extraordinary medical expenses' Name & amount-per-child-per-week column

facts.non_primary_pays_as_support.formula_text: Non-Primary Care Provider Adjustments (Amounts paid directly by Non-Primary Care Provider) Weekly health insurance (line 10) - $ Weekly child care (line 11) - $ Extraordinary Medical Expenses (line 12) - $ Non-Primary Care Provider pays as support = $
facts.non_primary_pays_as_support.note: line 14b: the printed '-' adjustments subtract from the Non-Primary Care Provider's support obligation down to the printed '=' result
